statusReason: Source, runtime URL, scrolled settings screenshots, candidate screenshots, multilingual Indonesian-main dictionary screenshots, and reverse lookup screenshot were captured. Settings screenshots are scrolled to expose lower controls including Candidates Jyutping, toggles, Reverse Lookup Settings, Show Full Input Code, and Cangjie Version.

browserCapture.browser: Google Chrome via Playwright Chromium launcher

screenshots.evidence: Runtime page scrolled down to expose lower settings and reverse lookup controls.
Controlled textarea input `nei` produced a visible candidate panel.
Runtime page with all Display Languages enabled and Indonesian selected as main, scrolled down to expose lower settings and reverse lookup controls.
Controlled textarea input `nei` with English, Hindi, Indonesian, Nepali, and Urdu enabled; Indonesian is the main language.
Scrolled down, then moved the mouse across the third candidate cell for 呢, showing canonical reference, written-form rows, literary reading note, part-of-speech, and lower More Languages details.
Typed `housam`, scrolled down, then moved the mouse across the second candidate cell for 好心你, showing compound dictionary entries for 好心 and 你.
Typed backtick + c + Cangjie code `onf` for 你, showing reverse lookup buffer `onf〔倉頡五代〕（人弓火）` and 你 as the first candidate.

runtimeObservation.dictionaryHoverBehavior: The Web alpha intentionally requires mouse movement over a candidate, not merely a stationary pointer, before showing the dictionary panel. Captured dictionary screenshots scroll down first, then move across concrete candidate cells for 呢 and 好心你.

settingsScrollCapture.reason: Settings screenshots are scrolled down so lower controls are visible: Candidates Jyutping, Auto-completion, Auto-correction, Auto-composition, Input Memory, Reverse Lookup Settings, Show Full Input Code, and Cangjie Version.

attemptedCommandsAndTools: Playwright Chromium launch with viewport 1280x720 and Chrome executable C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe
Playwright textarea input `nei`, screenshot capture for settings and candidate panel

Initial Playwright multilingual capture enabled all display languages, selected Indonesian as main language, typed `nei`, and moved the mouse over the first candidate cell before capturing the dictionary panel.

Playwright replacement capture selected the row containing third candidate 呢 and moved the mouse across its candidate cell before replacing dictionary-detail-multilingual-indonesian-main-desktop-1280x720.png.
Playwright recaptured both settings screenshots after scrolling to the lower settings area at window.scrollY 494.
Playwright captured `housam` after scrolling to window.scrollY 220 and moving across the second candidate cell for 好心你.
Playwright captured reverse lookup by pressing Backquote, then typing `conf` for Cangjie code `onf` of 你.
